title: Dicerothamnus rhinocerotis
taxonomy: Dicerothamnus rhinocerotis is a member of the family Asteraceae, which is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is further divided into the subfamily Gnaphaloideae and the tribe Gnaphalieae. The genus Dicerothamnus is endemic to South Africa, and D. rhinocerotis is one of the two species within this genus. Overall, the classification of D. rhinocerotis reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

properties: Dicerothamnus rhinocerotis, also known as the Rhino Bush, has been used in traditional medicine by the indigenous people of South Africa.
